column1: # Sample predictions for ARM B (OMC-style: ~40 skills ALWAYS resident).
# Format: <case-id><TAB><predicted-skill>   ("none" = the arm picked no skill).
# Illustrative sample data, NOT a measured run.
# The §1.1 thesis being probed: a large always-resident skill surface dilutes
# attention and supplies distractors, so the agent "occasionally latches onto
# the wrong one". This sample models that failure mode — more wrong-skill picks
# on near-misses (c12/c13/c15) and more false-triggers on decoys (c18/c19/c20)
# than the on-demand arm C — to show the metric that should separate B from C.
# Replace with real observations to get real numbers.
